name: Bpfdoor TCP Ports Redirect
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Rafal Piasecki
SIGMA Title: Bpfdoor TCP Ports Redirect
SIGMA Description: All TCP traffic on particular port from attacker is routed to different port. ex. '/sbin/iptables -t nat -D PREROUTING -p tcp -s 192.168.1.1 --dport 22 -j REDIRECT --to-ports 42392'
The traffic looks like encrypted SSH communications going to TCP port 22, but in reality is being directed to the shell port once it hits the iptables rule for the attacker host only.


False positives:
- Legitimate ports redirect

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=11 AND (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%iptables%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%-t%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a2%nat%') AND (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%--to-ports 42%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%--to-ports 43%') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Bpfdoor TCP Ports Redirect on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=11 AND (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a0%iptables%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a1%-t%' AND LOWER(Command) LIKE '%a2%nat%') AND (LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%--to-ports 42%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%--to-ports 43%') AQL filter query
